title: Uninitialized presale address allows anyone to mint tokens
reason: False positive: no transaction can originate from `address(0)`. A zero `_presaleCon` makes `mint()` unreachable, not publicly callable; that bricking is covered by F-001.

title: getFactor() calculation broken - token balances become incorrect after transactions
reason: The claim misunderstands the rebase accounting model. Large-balance conversions are intentionally handled via the factor and `_largeTotal`; the concrete bug here is missing initialization, already captured in F-001.

title: setTaxless() lacks access control - anyone can set taxless mode
reason: Incorrect: `setTaxless(bool)` is explicitly protected by the `onlyTaxless` modifier.

title: Division by zero in getFactor() if totalSupply becomes zero
reason: Too speculative as a standalone issue and not needed to explain realistic harm; the concrete deploy-time bricking from missing initialization is already captured in F-001.

title: createTokenPool uses block.timestamp as deadline - can fail immediately
reason: Using `block.timestamp` as a same-transaction router deadline is standard and does not by itself create a realistic exploit or loss scenario.

title: reassignTranche allows assigning to zero address
reason: Privileged misconfiguration/footgun only; no permissionless exploit or protocol-level failure is introduced.

title: silentSyncPair can be called on arbitrary addresses
reason: Public calls mainly expose the caller to a revert; the meaningful protocol issue is the internal use of zero `_mainPool`, captured in F-002.

title: addSupportedPool does not validate pairToken address
reason: Owner-only misconfiguration risk without a permissionless exploit path; not a reportable protocol bug on its own.

title: getUpdatedPoolCounters uses address(this) instead of pool address for token balance
reason: `IERC20(address(this)).balanceOf(pool)` is the correct way to read this token's pool balance. Problems from unsupported pools are already covered by F-002.

title: Using outdated Solidity version ^0.6.12
reason: Generic informational note, not a concrete vulnerability in this codebase.

title: No zero address validation in setLiquidityReserve and setStabilizer
reason: `AddressUpgradeable.isContract(reserve)` already rejects `address(0)`, so the specific zero-address claim is unsupported.
